title: $lines
text: $lines Return the number of lines remaining on the current page. $lines Example: if ($lines <10) ... Return Values Table 1. Values returned in $lines Value Meaning 0 Uniface is not printing ( $printing = 0); $status is set to an empty string (""). >0 Number of lines remaining on the page, excluding the header and footer frames. Uniface is printing ( $printing = 1). Use Use in Form and Report components. Description Use $lines to test whether there is enough space left to print your break frame, or to start printing a new occurrence on the current page. $lines is based upon how many lines of font 0 will fit on the page. If widget fonts specify different fonts or fonts size, the number of lines may be greater or less than this. If the number of lines is not a whole number, it is rounded downwards. For example 2.8 becomes 2. Thus, if $lines=2 there are between 2 and 3 lines of font 0 available. There is no function that can tell how many lines of a specified widget font will fit on a page. To print column headers on a new page, or text at the bottom of a page use headers and trailers surrounded by area frames with the Printing property set to Suppress when empty . This is easier then trying to do this with $lines . Example: Ejecting a Page The following example shows how to use $lines to trigger an eject if there is not enough room left to print information: trigger leavePrinted ; entity : INVOICE ; compare date of next occurrence compare (DATE) from "INVOICE" ; if next date different if ($result = 0) ; if less than 5 lines left, start printing on new page if ($lines < 5) eject endif ; set date in Break Frame DATE.DAYTOT = DATE.INVOICE ; print break frame for day total printbreak "DAYTOT" ; reset to zero for new day AMOUNT.DAYTOT = 0 ; if less than 10 lines left, eject after printing break frame if ($lines < 10) eject endif endif return(0) end; leavePrinted Copy Related Links Page feed after occurrence Determines how Uniface handles page breaks if there is not enough room to print a complete frame or occurrence.
